Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are going to construct an accepting Petri net for the given insurance claim process. Steps: 1. We will create a Petri net object. 2. We will define places for each state (and some intermediate places if necessary). 3. We will define transitions for each activity. 4. We will connect the places and transitions with arcs to model the process flow. 5. We will set the initial marking (a token in the start place) and the final marking (a token in the end place).

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process description: - The process starts with a claim (C). We can have a start place for the claim. - Then, Claim Registration (CR) occurs. - Then, Document Verification (DV) occurs. - After DV, there is a possibility of re-submission (RDS) if documents are incomplete. This is a loop between DV and RDS until documents are complete. - Then, Fraud Check (FC) occurs. - After FC, if doubts, then In-depth Investigation (II) occurs (possibly multiple times). This is a loop between FC and II. - Then, Claim Assessment (CA) occurs. - Then, Approval (AP) occurs if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. - Then, Payment (P) occurs. - Then, Notification (N) occurs. - Then, Closure (CL) occurs.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will model the process with the following places: - start (initial place, token for a new claim) - after_CR (after Claim Registration) - after_DV (after Document Verification, but before the decision for re-submission) - after_RDS (after Re-Document Submission, then goes back to DV) - after_FC (after Fraud Check, before the decision for in-depth investigation) - after_II ( after In-depth Investigation, then goes back to FC) - after_CA (after Claim Assessment, before the approval decision) - after_approval (after Approval, or after the skip approval path) - after_payment (after Payment) - after_notification (after Notification) - end (final place, token for closed claim)

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Steps in the Petri net: 1. start -> CR -> after_CR 2. after_CR -> DV -> after_DV 3. after_DV has two outgoing arcs: - one to RDS (if documents incomplete) - one to FC (if documents complete) We model this with two transitions from after_DV: transition DV_incomplete (which we can label as RDS) and transition DV_complete (which we can label as FC but note that FC is a separate activity, so we need a place for after_DV and then from after_DV we can go to either RDS or FC). However, note that the activity DV is already done. So after_DV is the state after DV. Then we have two possibilities: either the documents are complete (then we go to FC) or incomplete (then we go to RDS). We can model this by having two transitions from after_DV: - one transition labeled RDS (which goes to after_RDS, and then from after_RDS we go back to DV) - one transition labeled FC (which goes to after_FC, but note that FC is an activity, so we need a transition for FC).

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Alternatively, we can have two transitions from after_DV: - one transition (silent) for the complete case, which then leads to the FC transition. - one transition for RDS. But note: the process says after DV, if incomplete, then RDS occurs, then back to DV. So we have a loop: DV -> (if incomplete) RDS -> DV. We can model it as: after_DV -> (incomplete) RDS -> after_RDS -> DV -> after_DV (again) after_DV -> (complete) -> FC (but note FC is a transition, so we need to connect after_DV to the FC transition) However, we cannot have two transitions with the same label (unless we allow it, but we want distinct activities). So we can have: Transition for DV (from after_CR to after_DV) and then from after_DV we have two transitions: - one for RDS (which goes to after_RDS, then from after_RDS we go to the DV transition again? But then the DV transition would have two input places: after_CR and after_RDS).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets restructure: We can have a place for claim_needs_dv (which is the state before DV). Initially, after CR, we are in claim_needs_dv. Then we do DV and go to after_DV. Then from after_DV, if incomplete, we go to a place for claim_needs_rds, then do RDS, and then go back to claim_needs_dv. after_CR -> (arc to) claim_needs_dv claim_needs_dv -> DV -> after_DV after_DV -> RDS -> after_RDS after_RDS -> (arc to) claim_needs_dv after_DV -> (silent) complete -> claim_needs_fc

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets try to keep it simple: We can have: after_DV -> RDS -> after_RDS -> (arc to) place_before_DV after_DV -> FC -> after_FC This is acceptable because after DV, either we do RDS or we do FC. So the two transitions from after_DV are RDS and FC. Well do the same for FC and II: after_FC -> II -> after_II -> (arc to) after_FC after_FC -> CA -> after_CA

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now, for the approval step: after_CA -> (arc to) place_before_approval_decision Then, we have two transitions from place_before_approval_decision: - Approval (AP) -> after_approval - (silent) skip_approval -> after_approval But note: the process says the claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a threshold. We can model this by having two transitions: one for AP and one for skip_approval (silent). The token will be consumed by one of them.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets define the places and transitions: Places: start after_CR after_DV after_RDS after_FC after_II after_CA after_approval after_payment after_notification end Transitions: CR (label: Claim Registration) DV (label: Document Verification) RDS (label: Re-Document Submission) FC (label: Fraud Check) II (label: In-depth Investigation) CA (label: Claim Assessment) AP (label: Approval) skip_approval (silent) P (label: Payment) N (label: Notification) CL (label: Closure) Now, the arcs: start -> CR -> after_CR after_CR -> DV -> after_DV after_DV -> RDS -> after_RDS after_RDS -> DV -> after_DV after_DV -> FC -> after_FC after_FC -> II -> after_II after_II -> FC -> after_FC after_FC -> CA -> after_CA after_CA -> AP -> after_approval after_CA -> skip_approval -> after_approval after_approval -> P -> after_payment after_payment -> N -> after_notification after_notification -> CL -> end

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However, note that the loops are self-contained: the token after DV can either go to RDS or to FC. Similarly, after FC, the token can either go to II or to CA. We must also set the initial marking (im) to have one token in start, and the final marking (fm) to have one token in end.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets code accordingly. Well create the net, then create the places and transitions, then add arcs. Note: We are using the pm4py library. Well also need to import the necessary modules. Lets write the code step by step. Important: We must add the places and transitions to the net. Well use the petri_utils.add_arc_from_to function to add arcs. Lets code accordingly.
